item.title: Daily Briefing - 2026-06-25
item.description:
**Morning Briefing**
**What Changed Since Yesterday** Global markets opened mixed. The Dow is slightly up, while the Nasdaq is down. Oil prices continue to rise, now above $80 a barrel. Economic data releases are expected later this week.
**Venezuela Update** The Venezuelan government reported a modest increase in oil production. This comes amid ongoing negotiations for economic relief. Sanctions discussions have resumed between Venezuela and international actors. Watch for responses from the U.S. State Department.
**UK Response Signals** The UK government has reiterated its commitment to supporting Ukraine. Military aid discussions are still ongoing. Key ministers are expected to convene for a strategy meeting today.
**Legal/Docket Watch** No new developments reported in Epstein-related cases.
**What to Watch Today** 1. Economic data releases, particularly unemployment figures, will be closely monitored. 2. Statements from key officials regarding Venezuela. 3. Any announcements from the UK regarding military aid to Ukraine. 4. Market reactions to ongoing oil price fluctuations.
End of briefing.
